meta_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve no portfolio (Case Study)
content.seo_description: Learn how to use Git with AI to overcome a lack of a portfolio. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code to build your first project.
content.answer_block: Eliminate the 'no portfolio' barrier by using AI-assisted Git workflows. By pairing Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you can transform simple ideas into production-ready repositories. This approach proves technical competence through clean, documented commits, effectively demonstrating your ability to build real-world software.
content.intro:
You stare at the blank screen of an empty GitHub profile, the silence of your repository list echoing the anxiety of your job search. Every employer wants experience, but you cannot get experience without a project, and you cannot get a project without the confidence to start. The cycle feels unbreakable, a digital gatekeeping mechanism designed to keep you on the outside looking in.

But the era of the 'empty profile' is over. Today, you are not just a student; you are an AI-augmented builder. By leveraging modern CLI-based AI tools, you will bypass the paralysis of analysis and commit high-quality, architected code that speaks for itself. This case study is your blueprint to turning that empty profile into a professional portfolio that demands attention.

steps.title: Define the Project Scope
steps.description: Before coding, define a high-impact, small-scale project. We focus on a 'Task Aggregator' to demonstrate API handling and data persistence.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a senior software architect. Draft a project specification for a Task Aggregator web app. Include core features, a folder structure for a React/Node stack, and a plan for Git commit granularity.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Create a project README.md that outlines the technical stack, core features, and setup instructions for a Task Aggregator app.'

steps.title: Initialize and Scaffold
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate, ensuring professional project structure from the first commit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready boilerplate for a React frontend with Vite and a Node.js backend using Express. Ensure environment variables are structured correctly.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'npx create-vite@latest . --template react && npm install express cors dotenv'

steps.title: Implement Feature Iterations
steps.description: Build features incrementally. Each feature should be its own Git branch and commit, showing process maturity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a clean, modular function for the backend to handle task CRUD operations. Include error handling and JSDoc comments.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create a server.js file that implements GET, POST, and DELETE endpoints for tasks with proper status codes.'

steps.title: Automate Documentation
steps.description: Use AI to generate meaningful commit messages, turning simple code changes into a professional narrative.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current changes in the working directory and generate a conventional commit message following the format: type(scope): description.

steps.title: Final Review and Deployment
steps.description: Perform a final code audit to ensure the repository is ready for public display.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the repository for security vulnerabilities or hardcoded credentials. Suggest improvements for production readiness.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review the repository for any exposed secrets or code smells and suggest a deployment strategy for Vercel.'

pro_tips: Always use conventional commits (feat:, fix:, docs:) to show employers you understand team-based professional workflows.
Keep your README.md updated with screenshots; visual proof of a working UI is worth more than ten thousand lines of backend code.
Use AI to write unit tests for your core logic; having a 'tests' folder in your repo instantly puts you in the top 10% of junior applicants.
Branch your work by feature, not by date. It tells a story of how you solve individual problems rather than just dumping code.
Never hide your failures. If a feature was hard, document the 'why' in your PR description—it shows growth and self-awareness.
content.next_steps: After completing this project, deploy it to Vercel or Netlify and add the live link to your LinkedIn bio. Then, start your next project by building a different tool that solves a specific problem you face in your daily life.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the perfect moment to start your career. With AI, you have the leverage to build what used to take months in just a few days. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
